message:
test(ft-conformance): activation subprocess conformance for sigmoid/tanh/silu/mish [bd-2xxj]

Adds torch_activations_scipy_subprocess_conformance — the workhorse
activations behind binary classification (sigmoid), RNN/LSTM (tanh),
transformer FFN (silu = x*sigmoid(x)), and detection / vision
(mish = x*tanh(softplus(x))).

References:
  - sigmoid → scipy.special.expit
  - tanh    → numpy.tanh
  - silu    → numpy composition x * special.expit(x)
  - mish    → numpy composition x * np.tanh(np.log1p(np.exp(x)))

30 inputs covering negative / zero / positive / saturation
(±50, ±100, ±700) / transcendental constants / ±inf / NaN. ULP-
tolerant comparator: 4 ULP for sigmoid/tanh, 16 ULP for silu/mish
(composed paths accumulate one extra mul / softplus + tanh + mul
of rounding).

Saturation cases verify that sigmoid/tanh collapse cleanly without
overflow in the underlying exp — the standard concern for these
activations near ±large values.

Subprocess conformance count: 33. Closes frankentorch-2xxj.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -12,6 +12,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-2er","title":"Add custom autograd Function API (torch.autograd.Function equivalent)","description":"Users cannot currently define custom backward functions. This is arguably the single most impactful missing feature for real-world use — without it, any layer whose backward isn't built into FrankenTorch's op set is impossible to implement.

PyTorch's torch.autograd.Function API allows users to:
1. Define a custom forward() that registers itself on the autograd tape
2. Define a custom backward() that computes gradients for the custom op
3. Save tensors for backward via ctx.save_for_backward()

Implementation plan:
1. Add a CustomFunction trait in ft-autograd with forward/backward methods
2. Add a TensorNodeOp::Custom variant to the tensor tape
3. Add session.apply_custom_function() to ft-api that invokes the user's forward, registers the op on the tape, and stores the backward closure
4. During backward traversal, call the user's backward function when encountering Custom nodes
5. Handle save_for_backward by capturing TensorNodeIds in the closure context

Key design decisions:
- The backward closure must receive upstream gradient and return downstream gradients
- Must respect no_grad context (custom forward inside no_grad should not record on tape)
- Must work with the existing deterministic backward scheduler
- Error handling: user backward functions should return Result, not panic

Tests:
- Unit test: custom ReLU equivalent (forward: max(0,x), backward: 1 if x>0 else 0)
- Unit test: custom function with multiple inputs/outputs
- Unit test: custom function inside no_grad context (should not record)
- Unit test: custom function backward produces correct gradients (numerical gradient check)
- E2E test: train a model with a custom layer and verify loss decreases
- Edge case: nested custom functions
- Edge case: custom function that saves tensors for backward","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:46.006446323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:15:34.162735830Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:15:34.162441333Z","close_reason":"Core functionality already exists: tensor_apply_function() with FunctionCtx (save_for_backward, needs_input_grad) + backward closure. Extensive tests in ft-autograd (lines 17484+) and used internally by ft-api for ~12 ops. The closure-based API is the Rust-idiomatic equivalent of PyTorch's class-based torch.autograd.Function. A trait wrapper could be added later for API sugar but is not blocking.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2os","title":"Implement slogdet, pinverse, kthvalue, linalg_cross, linalg_vector_norm","description":"Add numerically stable linalg and selection ops: slogdet (sign+log determinant), pinverse (SVD-based pseudoinverse), kthvalue (k-th smallest), linalg_cross (3D cross product), linalg_vector_norm (flexible vector norm). Used in scientific computing, regularization, and model analysis.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-28T14:15:54.437563761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-29T02:09:46.045687331Z","closed_at":"2026-03-29T02:09:46.045431557Z","close_reason":"slogdet/cross already existed. Implemented pinverse (SVD-based), kthvalue (with index), linalg_vector_norm (L0/L1/L2/Linf). 9 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2rl","title":"Wire f32 tensor comparisons and float classify to dispatch","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T04:57:24.571301561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T05:01:04.720640114Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T05:01:04.720320760Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-2xxj","title":"Activation functions lack subprocess conformance: sigmoid, tanh, silu, mish","description":"Existing subprocess conformance suite covers gelu, softplus, exp/log/expm1/log1p/erf/erfc/lgamma/trig/hyperbolic, but the workhorse activations sigmoid / tanh / silu / mish have no scipy or libm reference comparison. PyTorch parity for these matters because:
- sigmoid (= 1/(1+exp(-x)) = expit) is the building block for binary classification, gating, attention
- tanh is the workhorse for RNN/LSTM
- silu/swish (= x*sigmoid(x)) is used in transformer FFN
- mish (= x*tanh(softplus(x))) is used in detection / vision

Reference: scipy.special.expit (sigmoid), numpy.tanh, numpy compositions for silu and mish. ULP-tolerant comparison; FrankenTorch composes these through libm exp/tanh which should match within ~1 ULP on smooth interior plus edge cases for ±inf / NaN / large-magnitude saturation.

Add torch_activations_libm_subprocess_conformance covering all four with ~30 inputs spanning negative / zero / positive / large / Inf / NaN. Filed during testing-conformance pass.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Added torch_activations_scipy_subprocess_conformance covering sigmoid (vs scipy.special.expit), tanh (vs numpy.tanh), silu = x*sigmoid(x), and mish = x*tanh(softplus(x)) across 30 inputs spanning negative / zero / positive / saturation (±50, ±100, ±700) / Inf / NaN. ULP-tolerant comparator: 4 ULP for sigmoid/tanh, 16 ULP for the composed silu/mish. Subprocess conformance count now 33 (was 32).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:09:45.897091490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:12:17.783694467Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:12:17.780442649Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-35e","title":"Update FEATURE_PARITY.md to honestly track unimplemented PyTorch subsystems","description":"FEATURE_PARITY.md currently marks 'Full PyTorch drop-in surface: parity_green' but omits major PyTorch subsystems from the tracking matrix entirely, creating a misleading picture.

What to add:
1. New section: 'Deferred Subsystems (not yet in scope)' with explicit not_started status for: GPU/CUDA backend, Distributed training (DDP/FSDP), JIT/TorchScript/torch.compile, Quantization, Sparse tensors, Mixed precision (AMP), ONNX export, Gradient checkpointing, Custom autograd Functions (torch.autograd.Function).
2. For each: document why it's deferred (e.g., 'CPU-first vertical slice, GPU planned for Phase 3+'), link to any future closure beads.
3. Change 'Full PyTorch drop-in surface: parity_green' to accurately reflect that it covers the CPU eager-mode subset only.
4. Add a 'Parity Scope Declaration' section that explicitly states what IS and IS NOT in scope for the current phase.

This is the single most important documentation fix — without it, the project appears done when it's at ~70% of PyTorch's core API surface.

Tests: N/A (documentation only). Verify by reading the updated file and confirming every major PyTorch subsystem is either tracked as green or explicitly listed as deferred.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:28.495902714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:04:43.553923709Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:04:43.553642487Z","close_reason":"Already completed by another agent: Parity Scope Declaration section added, Full PyTorch drop-in changed to parity_gap, Deferred Subsystems section with 9 entries and tracking beads, Gap Policy updated. Verified all referenced beads exist and tensor_apply_function claim is accurate.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-3cp","title":"Capture committed perf-SLO baseline artifact for G5","description":"Follow-up to frankentorch-vws.\n\nThe perf-SLO runner/gate now exists in ft-conformance, strict cargo gates pass, and we captured a verified single-sample full-profile measurement in artifacts/phase2c/performance/perf_slo_report_full_once.json plus PERF_SLO_SUMMARY_FULL_ONCE.md. The current result shows all five direct spec budgets failing and the three regression budgets remaining baseline_required without a committed baseline.\n\nRemaining work:\n1. Run the full perf-SLO profile with the intended statistical envelope (default 100 iterations, warmups as configured) on a suitable worker/host.\n2. Commit a stable baseline artifact at the canonical perf_slo baseline path for regression comparisons.\n3. Decide whether the single-sample full_once artifact should stay as an exploratory snapshot or be superseded by the committed baseline bundle.\n4. Hand the baseline path/contract to frankentorch-ioo so G5 can call run_perf_slos + check_perf_slos deterministically in CI.\n\nNotes:\n- rch exec validates runtime execution but does not automatically persist generated artifacts back into the local worktree across separate invocations, so baseline capture should use a workflow that intentionally preserves the produced artifact bundle.\n- Direct spec-budget failures from the single-sample full profile are already documented in artifacts/phase2c/performance/PERF_SLO_SUMMARY_FULL_ONCE.md.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:37.780725984Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T03:27:16.468792093Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T03:27:16.468370434Z","close_reason":"Normalized the canonical perf-SLO measurement/baseline paths, committed perf_slo_baseline_v1.json plus PERF_SLO_BASELINE_V1.md, and aligned runner/checker defaults so G5 has a stable baseline contract. Higher-iteration replacement baseline tracked separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-3u6","title":"Audit dispatch/kernel CPU edge cases","description":"Deep audit pass for ft-dispatch/ft-kernel-cpu execution flow; fix bugs/edge cases and add tests as needed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-11T03:24:33.376039097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T03:44:53.435483764Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T03:44:53.435310637Z","close_reason":"Fixed missing f32 test import; validated checks/tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
